claims.text: Rationalization about the future is senseless because a different future will actually change a person as an individual, altering how they evaluate products and technologies.
claims.speaker: Unknown
claims.mechanism: A realized future state changes the individual's identity and evaluation framework retroactively.

claims.text: Video conferencing adoption required an exogenous shock (major transport strike or minor pandemic) to overcome adoption friction; without such a shock, the technology would remain unused despite existing.
claims.speaker: Unknown
claims.mechanism: Forced necessity overcomes default behavior inertia; people will not voluntarily try existing technologies until circumstance forces adoption.
bounded_by: Applies to video conferencing specifically; may not generalize to all technologies

claims.text: Taking existing technologies and reframing them as appealing changes how people fundamentally evaluate the product.
claims.speaker: Unknown
conditions: audience has access to existing technology
claims.mechanism: Reframing alters evaluation heuristics; perception change drives trial.

examples.text: In 2019, before the COVID-19 pandemic, the speaker advised Zoom that to gain traction they would need either a major transport strike or a minor pandemic. The pandemic provided exactly that exogenous shock; Zoom adoption accelerated dramatically as people had no choice but to adopt video conferencing for work and social connection.
